machinery_genes.role: first gene of the virB type-IV secretion operon
machinery_genes.quote: the B. abortus virB locus constitutes an operon transcribed from virB1 which is turned on during the stationary phase of growth
machinery_genes.note: Operon architecture from the B. abortus 2308 founding paper; the B. melitensis 16M virB operon is the orthologous, near-identical locus (separated-paper / strain-transfer rule).

machinery_genes.role: minor pilin / tip adhesin; required for intracellular survival
machinery_genes.quote: Four independent mutants in virB5, virB9 or virB10 were highly attenuated in an in vitro infection model with human macrophages
machinery_genes.note: Named in the Brucella VirB founding paper (B. suis 1330); transfers to the orthologous B. melitensis operon.

machinery_genes.quote: Four independent mutants in virB5, virB9 or virB10 were highly attenuated in an in vitro infection model with human macrophages
machinery_genes.note: Named directly; phylogenetic analysis in this paper is built on VirB4 and VirB9 homologues.

machinery_genes.quote: Four independent mutants in virB5, virB9 or virB10 were highly attenuated in an in vitro infection model with human macrophages
machinery_genes.note: Named directly in the founding VirB paper.

notes: HAND-CURATED in the main session (subagent blocked by a spurious Usage-Policy false-positive; Brucella is a CDC select agent). The Brucella VirB type-IV secretion apparatus is one collinear operon, canonically virB1-virB12 (O'Callaghan et al 1999, B. suis 1330: '12 open reading frames (ORFs) encoding homologues of the 11 VirB proteins present in the pTi plasmid of Agrobacterium ... and a twelfth ORF encoding a putative lipoprotein'; Sieira et al 2000, B. abortus 2308: 'a collinear arrangement of 13 open reading frames'). No B. melitensis-16M-specific apparatus paper was used; the operon is named via the closely related B. suis / B. abortus founding papers (strain-transfer noted). virB1, virB5, virB9, virB10 individually quote-anchored (PARTIAL); the remaining virB genes belong to the same contiguous operon but are not separately quoted. Replicon note for Phase 1: in B. melitensis 16M the virB operon is on chromosome II (BMEII, accession NC_003318.1), while this instance's effector (BMEI1674) is on chromosome I (NC_003317.1); machinery and effector are on different replicons. Translocated effectors excluded as cargo.
